stem: A IT operations data backup uses 2 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability between 66% and 82% (uncertain due to limited operational data). storage device 2 has a reliability of 82%. What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9388 to 0.9676. Point estimate: 0.9532.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 storage devices fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.34 × 0.18 = 0.0612. P(works) = 0.9388.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.18 × 0.18 = 0.0324. P(works) = 0.9676.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
